mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 9.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and increasing retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand expansion, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO change × RSAFS change) shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a rising CPI index signals persistent inflation that erodes real corporate margins and forces further rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL rises alongside FEDFUNDS increases over a 2-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A continuous month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects sustained consumer confidence and construction activity, which supports broader economic growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for HOUST month-over-month changes against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized, it confirms a robust economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% compared to periods of sub-3.0% growth (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between rising industrial production and stagnant retail sales indicates supply chain bottlenecks outpacing consumer demand, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO change minus RSAFS change) fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in out-of-sample testing (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% year-over-year, a rising unemployment rate signals weakening demand that forces the Fed to pivot, which typically supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when UNRATE rises alongside CPIAUCSL > 3.5% YoY over a 2-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).
